channel.title: 简报 Jae Daily · 2026年06月30日
channel.description: 国际政治 · 宏观财经 · 前沿科技 · AI 辅助分析

item.title: [未译] Families of two football players killed and injured in Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Lucas Trejo of Argentina loses wife and two children while Héctor Bello’s partner dies saving their daughterFamily members of two professional footballers are among those killed by the twin earthquake

item.title: [未译] Trump's final appeal of E Jean Carroll sex abuse case rejected
item.description: The Supreme Court decision means the US president will now have to pay Carroll the $5m (£3.6m) in damages she had been awarded.

item.title: [未译] South Korea unveils $880bn chip and AI investment plan
item.description: It comes as regional rivals like Taiwan, China and Japan are investing heavily in chip factories and other technology.

item.title: [未译] Oil Holds Gain as Iran Seeks Hormuz Control Before Talks With US
item.description: Oil held an advance before expected US-Iran talks in Doha, as conflicting signals from Washington and Tehran cast doubt over the next phase of negotiations to end the four-month war.

item.title: [未译] Egypt Edges Closer to $1.6 Billion IMF Payout With Initial Deal
item.description: The International Monetary Fund and Egypt reached a preliminary deal on the country’s latest program review, putting Cairo on track to unlock more than $1.6 billion in financing.

item.title: [未译] Pakistan Says Strikes Kill 29 Afghans in New Border Flare Up
item.description: Pakistan said it carried out a ground operation and air strikes along its border with Afghanistan on Sunday and Monday, killing 29 militants in response to earlier attacks and marking the biggest flar

item.title: [未译] Yen Hits Four-Decade Low in Historic Slide That’s Rattled Japan
item.description: The yen slid to its weakest level against the dollar since 1986, a milestone that will generate unease in Japan and put traders on high alert for authorities wading into the market.

item.title: [未译] ‘Everyone is talking about Cape Verde’: World Cup run delights diaspora community in UK
item.description: Cape Verdeans in Britain feeling ‘incredibly proud’ after team’s hard-fought draws against Spain and UruguayWorld Cup live – latest updatesFor as long as she can remember, 13-year-old Lauryn struggled

item.title: [未译] Monaco explosion leaves three wounded as authorities probe deliberate attack
item.description: Three people were wounded in an explosion at a residential building in Monaco on Monday in what authorities said was a deliberate attack. The blast occurred around 9 p.m. (1900 GMT) on a street near t

item.title: [未译] South African leader warns anti-migrant protesters ahead of unofficial deadline
item.description: Thousands of people from other African countries have left South Africa ahead of Tuesday's deadline set by anti-migrant groups.

item.title: [未译] US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
item.description: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

item.title: [未译] Aftershock frays nerves as many Venezuelans left to fend for themselves
item.description: In areas devastated by the twin earthquakes, people are using crowbars, pickaxes and their bare hands to try to reach survivors.

item.title: [未译] Burnham's 'Manchesterism' could change the UK, but is not yet a full economic plan
item.description: Andy Burnham's speech depicted a different way of seeing and running the UK, though there are many other questions about the detail.

item.title: [未译] Ante: New Way to Blend Borrow Checking and Reference Counting
item.description: <p><a href="https://lobste.rs/s/vv4fhi/ante_new_way_blend_borrow_checking">Comments</a></p>

item.title: [未译] The Download: brain-melting heatwaves and unprecedented OpenAI restrictions
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Heat waves mess with your brain. Scientists are tr

item.title: [未译] Homes harder to sell as high mortgage rates frustrate buyers
item.description: Three in five homes listed for sale since January remain on the market, says property portal Zoopla.

item.title: [未译] We had packed lunches every day for 10 years and retired at 40
item.description: The Fire (Financially Independent, Retire Early) movement sees followers save as much as possible.

item.title: [未译] China Misses Out on AI Boom as Stocks Trail by Most Since 2001
item.description: After a banner year for Chinese stocks on the back of AI advances, 2026 is not going well.

item.title: [未译] Taiwan Dollar Set to Face Pressure From Record Dividend Payouts
item.description: The Taiwan dollar, which is on track for its first quarterly gain in a year, may face renewed pressure as the US currency strengthens and local companies boost dividend payouts to a record.

item.title: [未译] Raizen Burns More Cash, Sets Steeper Charge Amid Restructure
item.description: Brazilian sugar and fuels company Raizen SA reported a cash burn of 3.4 billion reais ($657 million) for the first three months of 2026 and roughly doubled its impairment charge for the crop year, sig

item.title: [未译] Putin makes rare admission of fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian strikes
item.description: The Russian president acknowledged Ukraine's attacks were "obviously creating problems" but denied the shortages were "critical".

item.title: [未译] Peru president-elect Fujimori vows 'order and hope' after narrow election victory
item.description: Peru's conservative president-elect Keiko Fujimori pledged on Monday to restore "order and hope" after narrowly defeating left-wing rival Roberto Sanchez in a presidential run-off dominated by surging

item.title: [未译] British American Tobacco to cut 9,000 jobs
item.description: The company had said earlier this year it was planning savings to make it "more digital and AI-focused".

item.title: [未译] Waymo and Uber quietly part ways in Phoenix
item.description: Uber said it is readying the launch of a separate autonomous vehicle partnership in the city, but did not name the partner.

item.title: [未译] Ford rehires human engineers after AI fails to match quality checks
item.description: The car-maker found AI quality checks failed to match the skill of veteran technicians.

item.title: [未译] Top Google Security Staff Warn Search Data Could Be Hacked if EU Rules Change
item.description: Europe’s pro-competition proposals could see Google Search and Android systems opened up. The company claims there are serious privacy flaws.

item.title: [未译] 'No one move!': The agonising silence as Venezuela rescuers listen for survivors
item.description: With tens of thousands of people still believed missing, teams listen for signs of life beneath the rubble.

item.title: [未译] Six people shot dead at centre for mothers and children in Germany
item.description: The male suspect who has been arrested was in a custody dispute over his baby daughter, police say.

item.title: [未译] AI agents are not your “coworkers”
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Imagine coming in to work to learn that a new underling will r

item.title: [未译] US parents charged with murder in death of 7-year-old son who weighed 116kg
item.description: The parents of a seven-year-old Michigan boy who weighed 116 kilograms (255 pounds) when he died have been charged with murder, torture and child abuse, prosecutors said. “Clearly the parents were fee
